behavior_summary: This script implements the NPC Gorgrond's interaction, examination, AI scheduling, theft response, fast-area triggers, and reaction to spells.
U8P_GORGROND::cast (process) marks Gorgrond as enraged (gorgrondMad = 3) and delegates to the combat spell loop (SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop) so the NPC reacts to being targeted by spells.

U8P_GORGROND::use (process) is the player-interaction entry: it opens/closes the NPC gump, begins a conversation and dispatches into several dialogue branches depending on global flags (acolyteKilled, gorgrondMad) and companion/alive checks.

U8P_GORGROND::func05A8 and U8P_GORGROND::func24D8 are the primary conversation/menu routines: they build and mutate option lists, capture player choices, set gorgrondMet on first meeting, and route to short monologues (U8P_GORGROND::func14F2, U8P_GORGROND::func17D7) or longer branches.

U8P_GORGROND::AvatarStoleSomething (process) applies a stepped theft response: increments gorgrondWarned, gives escalating spoken warnings and ultimately immobilizes and attacks the avatar (setAvatarInStasis, Npc::setTarget) when thresholds are exceeded or in an immediate-attack case.

U8P_GORGROND::look provides examine text variants (name/title/dead) controlled by gorgrondName and gorgrondJob. U8P_GORGROND::schedule, U8P_GORGROND::enterFastArea, and U8P_GORGROND::leaveFastArea provide periodic AI, movement initiation and cleanup behavior.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GORGROND::cast immediately writes gorgrondMad = 3 then calls SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop and suspends (combat/spell lifecycle handled externally).

U8P_GORGROND::use begins a conversation (BeginConversation), then chooses a dialogue helper: U8P_GORGROND::func05A8 (first meeting), U8P_GORGROND::func1A5E (angry branch), U8P_GORGROND::func1E03 (companion-alive random monologue), U8P_GORGROND::func24D8 (main conversation loop) or fallback U8P_GORGROND::func22C2.

Conversation helpers frequently U8P_GORGROND::use MenuSet/Ask/SuspendAssign and strcmp on captured answers to dispatch sub-branches; menus are repeatedly unioned/removed and reordered (FREE::moveToEndOfList) until the player chooses a farewell option.

U8P_GORGROND::AvatarStoleSomething reads and increments gorgrondWarned to step through verbal warnings and ultimately set the NPC's target and freeze the avatar; a local match (local1 == param1) can trigger immediate attack bypassing warnings.

U8P_GORGROND::schedule sets local timer/state values, calls external U8P_GORGROND::schedule helper METHOD::func143A, suspends, and on process_result() true calls METHOD::func133F — i.e., a sleep/wake follow-up pattern.
open_questions: What exact meaning/value space does the U8P_GORGROND::cast(uword) parameter hold (spell id, caster id, or effect type)? Inference: likely spell id/source (confidence: medium).
What external behavior does METHOD::func091C perform when called from U8P_GORGROND::AvatarStoleSomething (inventory recovery, bookkeeping, or quest state)? (low confidence).
Some numeric/global flags (e.g., ticked, gorgrondGroveled) are used locally — are they persisted globally across saves or meant to be transient counters? (medium confidence).
Precise side effects of METHOD::func133F, METHOD::func143A and METHOD::func15E7: they appear to manage scheduling/state changes but their full effects are external to this script (low confidence).

main_function.name: U8P_GORGROND::cast
main_function.purpose: Handle a spell-casting event targeted at Gorgrond: mark the NPC enraged and hand off to the combat spell lifecycle handler so the NPC responds appropriately to the spell/effect.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when a spell or combat magic effect is U8P_GORGROND::cast on or targeted at this NPC (process type 0x021D).
Takes a single uword parameter (likely identifies the spell or source).

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::cast
function_map.role: Spell-event handler that sets gorgrondMad and enters the combat spell handling loop (SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop).

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Theft response process: increments gorgrondWarned, issues staged warnings and can force attack/immobilize the avatar.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_GORGROND::look handler that returns short barks depending on name/job flags and death state.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic AI scheduler: prepares local U8P_GORGROND::schedule state, calls external U8P_GORGROND::schedule methods, and follows up after suspend/process_result().

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area trigger: initiates movement or sets target on avatar when conditions are met (not dead, avatarMasterSorcerer, not inDaemonScene).

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit cleanup; resets an internal reference via FREE::resetRef.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::use
function_map.role: Player-U8P_GORGROND::use entry: open/close gump, start conversation, and dispatch to dialogue branches based on flags and world state.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::func05A8
function_map.role: Initial greeting / first-contact conversation: builds menus, sets gorgrondMet, captures player choices and branches accordingly.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::func24D8
function_map.role: Primary conversation loop that builds dynamic menus, handles naming/farewell, and dispatches to smaller monologues.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::func1A5E
function_map.role: Angry/hostile conversation branch used when gorgrondMad is set; allows de-escalation or escalation to attack.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::func1E03
function_map.role: Randomized monologue chooser; includes grief/escalation behavior when acolyteKilled indicates a specific state.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::func14F2
function_map.role: Short farewell monologue with randomized variants and address token selection.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::func17D7
function_map.role: Short rude/insult monologue that increments gorgrondMad.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::func22C2
function_map.role: Contrite/polite monologue branch that sets gorgrondGroveled and calls external METHOD::func15E7 for side effects.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::func2497
function_map.role: Utility to reorder option lists (move a specific string to end) used when building menus.

internal_calls.purpose: Hand off to the combat-spell lifecycle and behavior while Gorgrond is under a spell/effect.
Route to initial greeting dialog when acolyteKilled == 0 or first-contact conditions apply.
Route to angry/hostile dialog if gorgrondMad is set.
Route to randomized monologue when a particular companion/context is present (SP-00h alive).
Route to the main conversation loop as a primary conversation branch.
Fallback rude response or short monologue when menus/options don't match explicit branches.
Adjust/reorder conversation menu before presenting it (e.g., move Farewell to end).
Finalization/follow-up after theft response sequence (exact effect unclear).

external_calls.purpose: External combat-spell loop handler that runs while the NPC is under a spell effect.
Initiate movement or pathing in response to a fast-area trigger when appropriate.
Clear internal reference associated with the fast-area (cleanup on exit).
Handle theft bookkeeping or inventory consequences early in the theft handler (inferred).
Freeze/immobilize avatar when theft escalation reaches attack threshold or immediate-attack condition is met.
Set Gorgrond's combat target to the avatar to begin attacking after escalation.
Open NPC gump/UI when player interacts.
Make Gorgrond hostile after repeated grief interactions (when ticked exceeds thresholds).
Reorder menu so Farewell appears last before presenting the Ask to the player.
Choose a random monologue variant when acolyteKilled isn't triggering the grief sequence.

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: Chooses grief/accusation vs. normal conversation branches in U8P_GORGROND::use() and U8P_GORGROND::func1E03.

reads.name: gorgrondMad
reads.likely_effect: Selects angry/hostile dialogue branches and variants; used to escalate or gate aggressive menu options.

reads.name: gorgrondName
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether examine/U8P_GORGROND::look output includes Gorgrond's full name and influences dialogue addressing.

reads.name: gorgrondJob
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether job/title is included in U8P_GORGROND::look/examination text and may add job-related menu options.

reads.name: inDaemonScene
reads.likely_effect: Alters U8P_GORGROND::schedule and U8P_GORGROND::enterFastArea behavior (skip movement/alternative scheduling in special scene).

reads.name: avatarMasterSorcerer
reads.likely_effect: If true in U8P_GORGROND::enterFastArea, causes Gorgrond to immediately set his target to the avatar (authority relationship).

reads.name: gorgrondDung
reads.likely_effect: Changes form of address to the insult 'Dungbreath' in dialogues that choose addressing token.

reads.name: gorgrondAvaName
reads.likely_effect: When set, causes the NPC to U8P_GORGROND::use the avatar's real name (getName()) in dialogues and menus.

writes.name: gorgrondMad
writes.likely_effect: Set to 3 by U8P_GORGROND::cast() (force enraged/hostile). Also incremented by insult/angry routines to escalate hostility or reset in de-escalation path.

writes.name: gorgrondWarned
writes.likely_effect: Incremented by U8P_GORGROND::AvatarStoleSomething to track theft warnings and determine when to escalate to attack.

writes.name: gorgrondMet
writes.likely_effect: Set on first contact in U8P_GORGROND::func05A8 so future greetings U8P_GORGROND::use the met-state and altered menu options.

writes.name: ticked
writes.likely_effect: Read/written in U8P_GORGROND::func1E03 to count grief interactions and determine when to escalate to attack (Npc::setTarget).

writes.name: gorgrondGroveled
writes.likely_effect: Set by U8P_GORGROND::func22C2 to mark that Gorgrond has given a contrite/apology response; prevents repetition of the longer apology variant.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the NPC Gorgrond's interaction, examination, AI scheduling, theft response, fast-area triggers, and reaction to spells.
